species: SMYD3, a member of the SET and MYND domain-containing (SMYD) family, is a histone methyltransferase (HMT) and transcription factor that plays an important role in transcriptional regulation in human carcinogenesis.
tissue: its expression is markedly upregulated in human liver cancer.
cell_type: to identify SMYD3-associated proteins in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) cells...
disease_state: SMYD3 promotes cell proliferation, invasion, and tumorigenesis in vivo and in vitro and found that its expression is markedly upregulated in human liver cancer.
sample_source: SMYD3 expression is upregulated in HCC tissues.
anatomic_site_tumor: its expression is markedly upregulated in human liver cancer.
cell_line: we investigated the role of SMYD3 in tumor development and progression in vivo by subcutaneously implanting LM3 cells...
sex: into immunocompromised severe combined immunodeficient (SCID) male mice (n = 5).
strain: subcutaneously injected into 6–8-week-old BALB/c nude mice.
